bug_report.summary: Small vertical marker/line artifacts differ near line starts in editor/preview content (possible indent-decorator rendering regression)
steps_to_reproduce: Look near the very start of the content in the left editor pane (around the YAML/frontmatter area).
Look at the left edge of the “# Helpful Links” heading in the lower-right pane.
expected_behaviors: No unexpected/extra vertical bars/markers should appear at the start/left edge of content unless they are intentional UI indicators, and their presence/position should be consistent between builds.
actual_behaviors: Thin vertical marker/line rendering differs between builds in two places: near the top of the left editor content (bbox [384,186,394,212]) and beside the “Helpful Links” heading area (bbox [895,538,905,584]).

bug_report.summary: After creating a new Markdown file, focus/placement of the new document is inconsistent: main editor stays on previous doc and new file opens in a different split pane
steps_to_reproduce: Open the app with split view panes visible.
Click Save in the save dialog.
Observe which editor pane/tab becomes active and where the new file is opened.
expected_behaviors: The newly created Markdown file should open in the currently active editor pane and become the focused/active document ready for typing (consistent behavior across builds).
actual_behaviors: Before-change: the new empty file opens as the active tab in the main (left) editor pane (status bar shows 0 words / 0 characters).

After-change: the main (left) editor pane shows the existing “Welcome to Zettlr!” content (status bar shows ~1,596 words / 9,023 characters), while the new empty file opens as a tab in the lower-right pane next to “Helpful Links,” effectively shifting the new document to a different pane and not keeping focus on it.

bug_report.summary: Active document indicators (word/character count) do not match visible main editor content after selecting the new-file tab in split view
steps_to_reproduce: Open the app with split view enabled (multiple editor panes visible).
Create a new Markdown file and save it (so an empty new document exists).
Click the new file’s tab (e.g., the timestamp-named tab) in the split pane as shown in the scenario.
Observe the main editor content and the word/character counters.
expected_behaviors: The UI should make it clear which document is active, and the global word/character counters should correspond to the document currently being edited/visibly active in the main editor pane (or the main editor pane should reflect the active document).
actual_behaviors: After-change: the counters show 0 words (suggesting the empty new document is active), while the main editor pane still displays the non-empty “Welcome to Zettlr!” content, creating a confusing mismatch between indicators and visible content (not observed in the before-change screenshot where 0 words matched an empty main editor).
